name: formal-gates
description: Agent Skill compatible AI code quality gate package. Core skill documents are runtime-neutral; bundled installer and hook targets are Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor. Go package validation is cross-platform. Hook enforcement must be verified per host with a live canary.

hosts.name: Claude Code
hosts.caveat: Install the installable skill subset as a Claude skill; command hook support requires configured settings and a live canary on Claude Code.

hosts.name: Codex
hosts.caveat: Install the installable skill subset as a Codex skill; hook enforcement requires Codex hook/config setup and a live canary on Codex. This package does not claim Codex plugin distribution.

hosts.name: Cursor
hosts.caveat: Install Cursor hook support through hooks.json; hook enforcement requires a live canary on Cursor.

hosts.name: Gemini
hosts.caveat: Gemini may use the readable workflow guidance if its environment supports equivalent instruction loading; this package ships no Gemini installer or hook target.

hosts.name: OpenCode
hosts.caveat: OpenCode may use the readable workflow guidance if its environment supports equivalent instruction loading; this package ships no OpenCode installer or hook target.

hosts.name: Windsurf
hosts.caveat: Windsurf may use the readable workflow guidance if its environment supports equivalent instruction loading; this package ships no Windsurf installer or hook target.

examples.purpose: Sample prompts for human or automated skill behavior review.

validation_notes: The built formal-gates native binary is the installed package and artifact validation entrypoint for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

The native canary command validates package shape, prompt pollution checks, hook decisions, workflow state, FinalExecution recording from a supplied artifact, receipt flow, and install shape without PowerShell.

Native workflow foundation commands exist for file-hash/git snapshot, record-stage, verify-admission, final-verification aggregation, FinalExecution recording from a supplied artifact, dry-run-first cleanup, and show, backed by the Go gate-state implementation and .claude/gates/gate-state.json.

Native receipt foundation commands exist for dispatch registration, lifecycle event capture, receipt finalization, receipt validation, and diagnostic preflight; missing lifecycle correlation remains unsupported or unproven, not PASS.
Native install copies the verifiable installable package subset without PS1/Python script runtime files and can write native hook configuration for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
Source checkout development tests may use go run ./cmd/formal-gates, but installed hook and validation paths must use bin/formal-gates(.exe).
The native Codex hook canary uses formal-gates canary codex-hook; same-host hook blocking still requires live canary evidence.
verification_commands contains only repo-local commands runnable from this package root.
support_caveats: Core skill documents may be read by other Agent Skill compatible runtimes, but only Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor have bundled installer/hook target entries.
Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode, and Windsurf are described by readable-skill, install-guidance, hook-configuration, and live-canary-proven blocking capability levels.
Hook enforcement claims require live canary evidence on the specific host.
A passing canary on one host does not prove another host. Do not claim Codex plugin distribution from this package.
release_trust: Phase 1 does not ship checksums, artifact attestation, npm provenance, signing, or equivalent release-trust evidence.
Phase 2 must define and implement release-trust evidence before public release claims rely on those controls.
